title: Multiweight homogeneity
summary: Assigns one ambient homogeneity to each input slot, sums the input weights, and defines the expected output weight after total Laplacian degree N.

mathematicalContent.heading: One weight per input
mathematicalContent.body: For F=(fᵢ), multi-homogeneity of weight vector w=(wᵢ) means that every component is homogeneous for the ambient Euler field X with its own weight.

mathematicalContent.heading: Total and output weights
mathematicalContent.body: The total input weight is the sum of the slot weights. An expression with total Laplacian degree N is expected to lower that sum by 2N.

mathematicalContent.heading: Low-rank expansions
mathematicalContent.body: For two and three inputs, the finite sums reduce to the usual pair and triple sums.

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Defines pointwise homogeneity of an input tuple: its ith function must have Euler weight wᵢ.
correspondence.leanType: def IsMultiHomogeneous {r : ℕ} (w : Fin r → ℝ) (F : CalConf.MultiInput r) : Prop

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Defines the total input weight as the finite sum of all slot weights.
correspondence.leanType: def totalWeight (_CalConf : Calculus Conf) {r : ℕ} (w : Fin r → ℝ) : ℝ

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Defines the expected output homogeneity after total Laplacian degree N by subtracting 2N from the total input weight.
correspondence.leanType: def outputWeight (CalConf : Calculus Conf) {r : ℕ} (w : Fin r → ℝ) (N : ℕ) : ℝ

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Extracts the ith component of a multi-homogeneity hypothesis.
correspondence.leanType: theorem IsMultiHomogeneous.apply {r : ℕ} {w : Fin r → ℝ} {F : CalConf.MultiInput r} (hF : CalConf.IsMultiHomogeneous w F) (i : Fin r) : CalConf.IsXHomogeneous (w i) (F i)

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Evaluates the total weight for a two-slot weight vector.
correspondence.leanType: theorem totalWeight_fin_two (w : Fin 2 → ℝ) : CalConf.totalWeight w = w 0 + w 1

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Evaluates the total weight for a three-slot weight vector.
correspondence.leanType: theorem totalWeight_fin_three (w : Fin 3 → ℝ) : CalConf.totalWeight w = w 0 + w 1 + w 2

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Unfolds the definition of expected output weight.
correspondence.leanType: theorem outputWeight_def {r : ℕ} (w : Fin r → ℝ) (N : ℕ) : CalConf.outputWeight w N = CalConf.totalWeight w - 2 * (N : ℝ)

references.note: Source for multilinear tangentiality and Laplacian-word constructions.

limitations: The arithmetic identities are proved, but homogeneity is expressed through the abstract Calculus.IsXHomogeneous predicate. outputWeight is bookkeeping only; this module does not prove that any concrete multi-operator actually has that output weight.
